questions.q: What type of monument is Rani ki Vav?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav, meaning 'The Queen's Stepwell', is an intricately constructed stepwell.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Rani ki Vav located?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav is situated in the town of Patan in the state of Gujarat.

questions.q: In which town is Rani ki Vav located?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav is located in Patan, the former capital of the Chaulukya (Solanki) dynasty.

questions.q: What does the name 'Rani ki Vav' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Rani ki Vav' translates to 'The Queen's Stepwell' in reference to its royal patron.

questions.q: Who built Rani ki Vav?
questions.explanation: The stepwell was commissioned by Queen Udayamati as a memorial.

questions.q: Rani ki Vav was built as a memorial to whom?
questions.explanation: Queen Udayamati built the stepwell as a memorial to her late husband, King Bhima I.

questions.q: Approximately in which century was Rani ki Vav originally built?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav was built in the 11th century during the Chaulukya (Solanki) period.

questions.q: What was the primary practical function of a stepwell like Rani ki Vav?
questions.explanation: Stepwells were built to store water and provide access to it, especially during dry seasons.

questions.q: Rani ki Vav is celebrated for being densely covered with what?
questions.explanation: The stepwell is famed for its hundreds of intricate sculptures and reliefs lining its walls and pillars.

questions.q: Rani ki Vav was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. To which organization does that designation belong?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav was added to UNESCO's World Heritage List in 2014.

questions.q: Queen Udayamati was the queen of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Udayamati was the queen of the Chaulukya (Solanki) dynasty that ruled Gujarat.

questions.q: Rani ki Vav was built in memory of which king?
questions.explanation: The stepwell was a memorial to King Bhima I, the Chaulukya ruler and Udayamati's husband.

questions.q: In what year was Rani ki Vav inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2014.

questions.q: On the banks of which river was Rani ki Vav built?
questions.explanation: The stepwell was constructed on the banks of the Saraswati River near Patan.

questions.q: Rani ki Vav is often described as which type of structure due to its layout?
questions.explanation: Because it descends underground with temple-like richness, it is described as an inverted temple.

questions.q: Which Indian banknote features an image of Rani ki Vav?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav appears on the reverse of the Indian 100 rupee note introduced in 2018.

questions.q: Many of the principal sculptures at Rani ki Vav depict the various forms of which deity?
questions.explanation: A large number of the stepwell's panels portray Vishnu, including his ten incarnations (Dasavataras).

questions.q: The set of ten incarnations of Vishnu prominently carved at Rani ki Vav is known as the:
questions.explanation: The Dasavatara, or ten incarnations of Vishnu, is a major sculptural theme at the stepwell.

questions.q: For a long period before its rediscovery, what had happened to Rani ki Vav?
options: It was buried under silt from flooding
It was converted into a mosque
It was moved stone by stone
questions.explanation: The Saraswati River flooded the stepwell, and it lay silted up for centuries before being excavated.

questions.q: Which agency is primarily responsible for the conservation of Rani ki Vav today?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) excavated, restored, and now maintains the monument.

questions.q: Approximately how many principal sculptural panels is Rani ki Vav famed for?
questions.explanation: The stepwell is renowned for over 500 principal sculptures along with many more minor reliefs.

questions.q: Patan, the location of Rani ki Vav, historically served as what?
options: The capital of the Chaulukya dynasty
questions.explanation: Patan (Anhilwad Patan) was the capital of the Chaulukya (Solanki) rulers of Gujarat.

questions.q: Rani ki Vav is built in which architectural style associated with western Indian temples?
questions.explanation: It exemplifies the Maru-Gurjara architectural style characteristic of the Solanki period in Gujarat and Rajasthan.

questions.q: Into how many levels or storeys does Rani ki Vav descend?
questions.explanation: The stepwell descends through seven levels of stairs and sculptured panels toward the well.

questions.q: King Bhima I, in whose memory the stepwell was built, was the father of which later Chaulukya ruler?
questions.explanation: Bhima I was succeeded by his son Karna (Karnadeva), continuing the Chaulukya line.

questions.q: Many female figures carved at Rani ki Vav are graceful celestial maidens generally referred to as:
questions.explanation: The walls feature numerous apsaras (celestial nymphs) shown in elegant poses and adornments.

questions.q: Under which UNESCO criterion category does Rani ki Vav qualify as a World Heritage Site?
options: A mixed natural and cultural site
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav is inscribed as a cultural World Heritage Site for its architectural and artistic value.

questions.q: The deepest part of Rani ki Vav, where water collected, is best described as:
questions.explanation: At its western end the structure terminates in a deep cylindrical well shaft that held the water.

questions.q: The Maru-Gurjara style seen at Rani ki Vav is also associated with which other famous Solanki-era Gujarat monument?
questions.explanation: The nearby Modhera Sun Temple shares the same Solanki-period Maru-Gurjara architectural tradition.

questions.q: In 2016, under a Government of India survey, Rani ki Vav received which distinction?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav was declared the 'Cleanest Iconic Place' in India under the Swachh Bharat survey in 2016.
